title: Number of people living in extreme poverty
subtitle: Extreme poverty is defined as living below the [International Poverty Line](#dod:international-poverty-line) of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Number of people living in extreme poverty

Extreme poverty is defined as living below the International Poverty Line of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

The chart displays global population trends from 1990 to 2026, divided into two stacked areas representing those living in extreme poverty and those not in extreme poverty. The lower section, colored in dark pink, tracks the number of people living in extreme poverty. In 1990, this value sits slightly above 2 billion. The line remains relatively flat through the mid-1990s before beginning a steady decline around the year 2000. By 2010, the figure drops to approximately 1.5 billion, continuing downward to reach just under 1 billion by 2020. The trend stabilizes in the final years, hovering near the 1 billion mark through 2026. The upper section, colored in light orange, represents the population not in extreme poverty. This area expands significantly over the period, starting at roughly 3.3 billion in 1990 and rising continuously to exceed 7 billion by 2026. The total height of the chart, representing the global population, grows from approximately 5.3 billion in 1990 to over 8 billion in 2026. The data is expressed in international dollars at 2021 prices and relates to income or consumption per capita depending on the country and year.
